---
title: "tickfat"
output: html_document
date: "2024-10-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

#install packages
if (!require("tidyverse")) install.packages("tidyverse")
if (!require("googlesheets4")) install.packages("googlesheets4")
if (!require("janitor")) install.packages("janitor")
if (!require("lubridate")) install.packages("lubridate")
if (!require("readxl")) install.packages("readxl")
if (!require("granova")) install.packages("granova")
if (!require("survminer")) install.packages("survminer")
if (!require("survival")) install.packages("survival")
#set working directory to present directory
setwd(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))
getwd()

#load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(googlesheets4)
library(janitor)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)
library(granova)
library(survminer)
library(survival)


#for making reports
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
```{r import}
df_raw <- 
  read_excel("../data/tickfat_master.xlsx", 
    sheet = "weigh_data") |>
 clean_names()

df_raw <- as_tibble(df_raw)
```
```{r google import}
df_raw <- suppressWarnings(
  read_sheet(
    "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10bdLgXpviS8Z4QTISbPmXtB9WXn0-Dc_dnBluMmYFmI/edit?usp=sharing", 
    sheet = "weigh_data"
    )
) |> clean_names()
```
```{r data wrangle}
#remove bad data
df_raw[61,15] = NA

df_wrangled <- df_raw |>
  #rowwise allows taking a mean across columns
  rowwise() |>
  #c_across is necessary for taking a mean across columns
  mutate(
    dess_wt_mean = mean(c_across(starts_with("dess_wt")), na.rm = TRUE), 
    dess_wt_sd = sd(c_across(starts_with("dess_wt")), na.rm = TRUE), 
    .after = dess_wt_4
  ) |>
   mutate(
    xt_wt_mean = mean(c_across(starts_with("xt_wt")), na.rm = TRUE), 
    xt_wt_sd = sd(c_across(starts_with("xt_wt")), na.rm = TRUE), 
    .after = xt_wt_3
  ) |>
  mutate(
    body_fat_raw = dess_wt_mean-xt_wt_mean,
    pct_bf = body_fat_raw/dess_wt_mean, 
    ) |>
  #regex to pull first digit out of id column --> treatment
  mutate(
  treatment = str_match(tick_id, "[A-Z]([0-9]).*")[,2],
  .after = tick_id
) |>
  mutate(treatment = if_else(treatment == "0" & time == 0, "1", treatment))

  
```
```{r plots}
fatplot <- df_wrangled |>
  filter(dead == 0) |>
  filter(!is.na(sex_m_f)) |>
  filter(pct_bf >= 0) |>
  ggplot(aes(y = pct_bf*100, 
             x = as.factor(time))) +
  labs(#title = "Body fat percentage vs. sex",
       y = "Pct body fat",
       x = "Time")
      
fatplot_scatter <- fatplot +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~treatment)
fatplot_scatter

fatplot_box <- fatplot +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = sex_m_f)) +
  facet_wrap(~treatment, 
             nrow = 1,
             labeller = labeller(treatment = c(
               "1" = "RH 85%",
               "2" = "RH 70%",
               "3" = "RH 60%",
               "4" = "RH 50%",
               "5" = "RH 40%"
             ))) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "E", name = "sex", begin = 0.55, end = 0.95) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
   strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#FFF2CC", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
   strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"), 
   #strip.background = element_blank()
   ) 
fatplot_box



```
```{r summary tables}
summ <- df_wrangled |>
  filter(dead == 0) |>
  filter(!is.na(sex_m_f)) |>
  filter(pct_bf >= 0) |>
  group_by(treatment, sex_m_f,time) |>
  summarize(
    n=n()
  )
summ

```
```{r stats}
#hypothesis:  Male and Female ticks have different mean body fat percentage

fm_plot <- df_wrangled |>
  filter(dead == 0) |>
  filter(!is.na(sex_m_f)) |>
  filter(pct_bf >= 0) |>
  ggplot(aes(x=sex_m_f, y = pct_bf))+
  geom_boxplot()

fm_plot

df_stat <- df_wrangled |>
  filter(dead == 0) |>
  filter(!is.na(sex_m_f)) |>
  filter(pct_bf >= 0) 

 t.test(pct_bf ~ sex_m_f, data = as.data.frame(df_stat))
 

#1-way ANOVA: 
#hypothesis:  Male and Female ticks have different mean body fat percentage
 
aov.tick <- aov(pct_bf ~ sex_m_f, data = df_stat)
summary(aov.tick)

granova.1w(data = df_stat$pct_bf, group = df_stat$sex_m_f,  h.rng = 1.6, v.rng = 0.5, top.dot = .35)

#hypothesis: Fat decreases over time in humidity treatment 1

df_stat_1 <- df_stat |>
  filter(treatment == 1)

aov.tick <- aov(pct_bf ~ time, data = df_stat_1)
summary(aov.tick)

#2-way anova, testing effects of time and sex on pct_bf
aov.tick <- aov(pct_bf ~ time + sex_m_f, data = df_stat_1)
summary(aov.tick)

#2-way anova, testing effects of time and sex on pct_bf, with interactions
aov.tick <- aov(pct_bf ~ time * sex_m_f, data = df_stat_1)
summary(aov.tick)

#2-way ANOVA:
#hypothesis: mean body fat percentage differs by sex or time
#note interaction term here (sex * treatment)

aov.tick <- aov(pct_bf ~ sex_m_f * treatment, data = as.data.frame(df_stat))
summary(aov.tick)

#3-way ANOVA:
#hypothesis: mean body fat percentage differs by sex or time or humidity

aov.tick <- aov(pct_bf ~ sex_m_f + treatment + time, data = as.data.frame(df_stat))
summary(aov.tick)

#2-way ANOVA:
#hypothesis: mean body fat percentage differs among treatments at time 1

df_stat_2 <- df_stat |>
  filter(time==1) |>
  filter(treatment %in% c(1:5))

aov.tick_txmf <- aov(pct_bf ~ treatment + sex_m_f, data = as.data.frame(df_stat_2))
summary(aov.tick_txmf)
AIC(aov.tick_txmf)

aov.tick_txmf_i <- aov(pct_bf ~ treatment * sex_m_f, data = as.data.frame(df_stat_2))
summary(aov.tick_txmf_i)
AIC(aov.tick_txmf_i)

```
```{r survival analysis}
#vignette
fit <- surv_fit(Surv(time, status) ~ rx,
               data = colon,
               group.by = "sex"
)
               
ggsurvplot(fit, data = colon, facet.by = "age")    

surv_pvalue(fit)

cfit1 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ rx + sex, data = colon)
cfit1
extractAIC(cfit1)

cfit2 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ rx * sex, data = colon)
cfit2
extractAIC(cfit2)

#create data frame with no NA an no Treatment 0
df_surv <- df_wrangled |>
  dplyr::filter(is.na(omit)) |>
  dplyr::filter(treatment != 0)

#fit Kaplan-Meyer curves
fit3 <- surv_fit(Surv(time, dead) ~ treatment, data = df_surv)

#plot survival
ggsurvplot(fit3, 
           data = df_surv, 
           fun = "pct", 
           pval = TRUE,
           xlab = "month",
           break.x.by = 1,
           linetype = "treatment",
           alpha = 0.5
           )

surv_pvalue(fit3)

#converted to barchart from fit object
tibble(time = fit3$time, surv = fit3$surv, treatment = c(1,1,1,2,2,2,3,3,3,4,4,4,5,5,5)) |>
  ggplot(
    aes(x = treatment, y = surv, group = time, fill = time)
    ) +
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity", 
    position = "dodge"
    ) +

  labs(
    title = "Percent survival at 4 wk",
    subtitle = "High to low humidity", 
    x = "humidity treatment", 
    y = "survival"
  )

#multivariate analysis using Cox Proportional Hazards
cfit3 <- coxph(Surv(time, dead) ~ treatment + sex_m_f, data = df_surv)
cfit3
extractAIC(cfit3)

cfit4 <- coxph(Surv(time, dead) ~ treatment * sex_m_f, data = df_surv)
cfit4
extractAIC(cfit4)

#check for proportional hazards

checkph <- cox.zph(cfit3)
checkph

```
